language_issues.title: Verb 'Gustar' Structure with Indirect Object
language_issues.description: Learner uses English-like subject patterns with 'gustar' instead of the Spanish indirect-object structure.

theory_voice.spoken_explanation: In Spanish, with 'gustar', the person is not the subject. You say 'a' plus the person, and you repeat it with 'me', 'te', 'le', or 'les'. The thing you like is the subject.

example_pairs_audio.correct: Creo que a ella le gustará.

exercises.agent_prompt_spoken: Say in Spanish: I think she will like the game design book.

grading.semantic_target: Creo que a ella le gustará el libro de diseño de juegos

on_fail.text: Listen carefully. With 'gustar', you need 'a ella' before 'le gustará'. Try again.
Repeat after me: Creo que a ella le gustará el libro.

spoken_feedback.on_pass: Good. You used 'a ella' and 'le' correctly.

exercises.agent_prompt_spoken: I will say two sentences. Say which one sounds correct: first or second.

audio_pairs.first: Creo que ella le gustará la app.
audio_pairs.second: Creo que a ella le gustará la app.

spoken_feedback.on_fail: The correct one includes 'a ella'. Let's say it together.

exercises.agent_prompt_spoken: Correct this sentence out loud, keeping the same meaning: Creo que ella le gustará esta herramienta.

spoken_feedback.on_fail: You only need one change. Add 'a ella' before 'le gustará'.

exercises.agent_prompt_spoken: Complete the sentence out loud: A mí ___ gusta esta idea de startup.

spoken_feedback.on_fail: Remember, it's 'a mí me gusta', not 'yo me gusta'.

exercises.agent_prompt_spoken: Say in Spanish: I don't think they will like these notes.

grading.semantic_target: No creo que a ellos les gusten estas notas

common_error_patterns.diagnosis: subject used instead of indirect object

on_fail.text: You need 'a ellos les' and the plural verb 'gusten'. Try again.
Repeat after me: No creo que a ellos les gusten estas notas.
